records.Transcript_Notes: Court record locates John Greene Sr. at Narragansett/Aquidnesitt. Solidifies residence prior to King Philip's War.
Names Anashuecot 'chiefe Sachem and heire properly'. All 7 signers marked 'his marke' — scribal construct, not grammatical gender in Narragansett.

records.Source_ID: RI Land Evidences Vol. I, p.49
records.Transcript_Notes: Female sachem correctly gendered in 1674 — two years AFTER 1672 Anashuecot 'his marke'. Same scribal pool, different outcome. John Greene witness. Amos/Samponock alias. Interpreters present.
Eldest son confirms mother's Succonett sale. John Greene signs 'his marke'. Thomas Jeffery witness. Reveals third sibling in sachem-line family.

Phonetic_Variations: Mr R. Smiths John Wall Maker
records.Transcript_Notes: Roger Williams letter tracks specialized, liminal Indigenous/English figures in trading post orbit. Confirms systematic cultivation of bilingual/liminal Indigenous figures within Smith's domain.
Major John Talcott's dispatch: Second Battle of Nipsachuck. Quaiapen slain. Hard chronological firewall — decouples Quaiapen from Anashuecot.

records.Source_ID: Records of the Colony of RI & Providence Plantations, Vol. 2, pp.62-63
records.Transcript_Notes: John Greene named as Deputy from Warwick to King's Council in London. Negotiating Rhode Island sovereignty over Narragansett Country. Highest-level colonial diplomatic record.

records.Source_ID: Records of the Colony of RI & Providence Plantations, Vol. 2, pp.56-57
records.Transcript_Notes: Greene swears to 40+ years at Cocumscussoc, witnessing Narragansett Indian assemblies. Entered records alongside Roger Williams testimony (July 24).
180 acres to Daniel (120ac) and James (60ac). Life-estate clause: '...as long as their father or mother should live'. Confirms Joan alive.

records.Source_ID: Records of RI & Providence Plantations, Vol. VII, pp. 177-178
records.Transcript_Notes: Joan's 'free & voluntary consent' to £10 sale to William Carpenter. First direct physical record of Joan's active legal agency via signature mark.

records.Source_ID: Bates, RIHS Collections XI (1918), read directly
records.Transcript_Notes: John Greene Sr. proven dead before 25 September 1685. Nullifies ALL theories dependent on 1695 date.
John Sr.'s will lost in North Kingstown town fire. Explains severe lack of explicit probate lists detailing Joan's background.
Clerk Fones enters 1672 deed AND 1677/8 affidavit into official record. 31 years post-execution. Isolated act separate from main 1686 campaign.
8th phonetic variant of Anashuecot. 'Daniel Green' witness in court-martial records.
Narragansett Tribal List includes 'Green' surname. Confirms historic convergence of surname within tribal sovereign structure.
F.L. Greene: 'His wife, in 1682, was named Joan, but nothing further is known...' Historical boundary of traditional print research.
Bates publishes 'John Greene of Newport and Narragansett'. Highly rigorous. Structural core of Five-John Firewall.
